title: Consolidated Solutions

source.file: input_data/corpus/20200803 PC-Strategy-Solution Development and GoToMarket PlanVer1.5.xlsx

grain: one row within a solution block; each solution offering (in 'Offering') spans several stacked rows that list its modules and its EXL capability-area ratings, with solution-level fields populated only on the block's lead row
summary: A catalogue of proposed solution offerings, each described by its problem statement, component modules, target outcomes, metrics/benchmarks, buying center, and client/market appetite, plus an EXL-capability self-assessment (capability area, an L/M/H rating, and details) and next steps / speed-to-market. It lets you look up what each offering is, why it matters, who buys it, and how ready EXL is to deliver it. The layout is a multi-row block per offering, so offering-level fields appear only on each block's first row.
use_cases: What solution offerings are proposed and what problem does each address?
For a given offering (e.g., FNOL 'As a Service'), what modules, outcomes, and metrics/benchmarks are listed?
Which buying center, client appetite, and market appetite are noted for each offering?
What EXL capability areas (Operational/Platform/Analytics/Digital) and L/M/H readiness back each offering?
What are the next steps and speed-to-market for each offering?

columns.note: solution block sequence number; populated on lead row only

columns.name: Offering
columns.note: solution/offering name; populated on each block's lead row

columns.name: Modules
columns.note: one module per row; the most row-level field

columns.name: Buying Center
columns.note: target buyer role, e.g. Chief Claim Officer

columns.name:
Client Appetite / Feedback
Feedback received from clients to be updated here
columns.note: free-text list of interested client names

columns.name: Existing Capability in market (are there any existing similar products in the market?).  Describe.
EXL Capabilities
columns.note: the 'Area' sub-column of the two-row 'EXL Capabilities' header: Operational/Platform/Analytics/Digital

columns.name: Unnamed: 11
columns.note: L/M/H rating for the EXL capability area (row-2 sub-header 'L/M/H')

columns.name: Unnamed: 12
columns.note: Details for the EXL capability area (row-2 sub-header 'Details')

examples.Problem Statement: The first step in the Claim process is FNOL and it is one of the most important. It is often the first interaction a policyholder has with their insurance carrier and it represents a Moment of Truth for the carrier...[truncated]
examples.Modules: 1. Omni Channel Intake - provides a channel agnostic claim reporting solution for customers (broker and insured)
examples.Outcomes: -Reduce claim costs - ALAE and ULAE; -Streamline and simplify the process; -Improve accuracy of assignment; -Provide early warning flags for fraud, litigation...[truncated]
examples.Metrics and Benchmarks: TAT Non Voice - 95% assigned within 2 hours (most stringent); AHT - not always measured; Quality - 88%-95% DPU and DPO...[truncated]
examples.Client Appetite / Feedback\nFeedback rec: Allstate, Assurant, CNA, Hanover, HIG, Liberty Mutual
examples.Existing Capability in market (are there: More research needed here. No one has tied the whole solution together. We are creating a one stop shop with the ability to provide data loops
examples.Unnamed: 12: Moderate footprint in FNOL both voice and non-voice across personal and commercial insurance
examples.Speed to Market: Medium. We can package and put Value Prop together quickly. Building and stitching the modules together will take more time.
examples.Next Steps: 1. Vet the solution concept and components with existing clients 2. If prioritized, begin to build the Packaged Solution collateral including the Value Prop

examples.Modules: 2. Automated Entry FNOL capability (Thin Human/Heavy Digital) - provides front end capability to ingest all types of FNOL documents across all lines of business...[truncated]

not_answerable.q: What is the revenue or price of each solution offering?
not_answerable.reason: no financial/pricing column exists in this table

not_answerable.q: How has appetite for an offering changed over time?
How do named competitors rate against each offering?
not_answerable.reason: no competitor columns in this sheet

warnings: two-row header band: row 1 group headers + row 2 sub-headers (Area / L/M/H / Details under 'EXL Capabilities'); profiler-detected header_row corrected to 1
multi-row block layout: solution-level fields (#/Offering/Problem Statement/Outcomes/etc.) populated only on each block's lead row, blank on continuation rows
near-duplicate of sheet 'P&C Solutions List' (identical column set and values); this sheet kept as canonical
'EXL Capabilities' L/M/H and Details live in the 'Unnamed: 11'/'Unnamed: 12' columns due to the merged two-row header
